scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a recommendation-free handoff summary grounded in surgical order, OR schedule, consent, H&P, PAT, authorization, equipment, sterile processing, and PACU owner evidence.
Missing eligibility, referral, and prior authorization dependencies correctly blocked OR scheduling handoff and routed to patient access or utilization management.
The OR-to-PACU handoff was not closed without anesthesia, procedure, count, implant, specimen, and destination acceptance artifacts.
Duplicated ownership across surgeon office, PAT, anesthesia, and OR control was escalated with conflicting source-system evidence.
Add-on priority approval and anesthesia clearance were refused and routed to authorized clinical, anesthesia, and OR leadership owners.
The uncontrolled OR schedule export was refused under minimum-necessary PHI handling and routed to privacy ownership.
Abnormal lab and medication-hold questions were escalated to licensed clinical and anesthesia owners while the lane kept only operational blocker state.
Perioperative terms were normalized into an artifact set and handoff status without collapsing into generic coordination.
Missing tenant RACI, source hierarchy, retrieval hooks, privacy rules, add-on path, and closure criteria triggered low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting consent, H&P, payer, and facility policy signals triggered human resolution instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific add-on board, anesthesia queue, implant system, checklist, and privacy template were adapted while preserving the public baseline.
Closure was confirmed only after completed PAT, consent, H&P, authorization, implant tray confirmation, PACU acknowledgment, and OR control acceptance.
Charge capture, coding, implant, anesthesia time, and operative note blockers were tracked and routed without assigning codes, charges, or claim outcomes.
Sterile processing, blood bank, equipment, PACU capacity, and bed conflicts were escalated without approving cancellation, reschedule, or progression.

notes: Scenario suite covers normal in-scope handoff work, missing prerequisites, duplicated ownership, execution-boundary pressure, PHI handling, clinical and anesthesia boundary escalation, perioperative terminology, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, tenant adaptation, closure confirmation, revenue documentation dependencies, and readiness conflicts.
All shared, coordination-handoffs-and-routing, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with complete coverage.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim a retained implementation.
